howItWorks.intro: This irrigation calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: 1 inch of water per 1,000 sq ft = 623 gallons
Most vegetables need 1-1.5 inches per week
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much should I water my garden?
faq.answer: Most vegetables need 1-1.5 inches of water per week. In hot weather, increase to 2 inches. Water deeply 2-3 times per week rather than lightly daily — this encourages deep root growth.

faq.question: When is the best time to water?
faq.answer: Early morning (6-10 AM) is ideal. Water evaporates less, leaves dry before evening (reducing disease), and plants are hydrated for the hottest part of the day. Avoid watering in the evening — wet leaves overnight promote fungal disease.

faq.question: How do I know if I'm overwatering?
faq.answer: Signs: yellow leaves, wilting despite wet soil, root rot (mushy roots), fungus gnats, mold on soil surface. Stick your finger 2 inches into soil — if it's still moist, don't water. Most plants prefer slightly dry to constantly wet.

faq.question: Is the Irrigation Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard gardening and horticulture formulas trusted by gardeners and growers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Irrigation Calculator?
sections.body: The Irrigation Calculator is a free online tool designed for gardeners and growers who need quick, accurate calculations in the gardening and horticulture space. By entering your garden area, weekly water need, flow rate, you get instant results including gallons per week, runtime needed, monthly water cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting gallons per week right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In gardening and horticulture, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by gardeners and growers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to grow a thriving garden with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your garden area and need to find the right gallons per week. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
